description_long: This object is a traditional harp with a rustic, handcrafted appearance, featuring a wooden frame and a rectangular resonator body. The harp has a gently curved vertical column with tuning pegs protruding from one side, each holding a taut string that extends diagonally to the soundboard. The base of the harp is solid and block-like, likely made from a single piece of wood, and shows signs of wear and age, with a rough, matte finish. The strings are thin and evenly spaced, anchored at the top of the column and the lower soundboard. There are no visible decorative carvings or inlays, giving the instrument a utilitarian and functional look. The overall coloration is a mix of natural wood browns and muted beige tones, with some darker areas from aging or use.
description: A rustic, handcrafted wooden harp with a gently curved column, rectangular resonator, and seven visible strings. The instrument has a worn, matte finish and a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The harp is seen from a side angle, clearly showing the curved column, all seven strings, and the rectangular wooden base.
description_view_1: A front-on view highlights the alignment of the tuning pegs and the narrow profile of the harp's body.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the curve of the column and the full length of the strings, with the base and soundboard visible.
description_view_3: A rear view focuses on the back of the column and the rectangular base, with tuning pegs extending outward.
attribute_frame_design: The frame features a gently curved vertical column with a rectangular base, resembling a traditional arched harp with a simple, utilitarian construction.
attribute_column_curvature: The column is gently curved, arching smoothly from the base to the top where the tuning pegs are located.
